mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. The signal activates only when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a rapid monetary tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and continues to rise, equity risk premiums expand as borrowing costs outpace earnings growth, suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: rapid monetary tightening cycle with rates > 2.0%
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or statistically neutral when FEDFUNDS is above 2.0% and rising, or the slope coefficient of FEDFUNDS on forward returns becomes positive in a rolling regression window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances reflect expanding household purchasing power and economic momentum, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, it indicates a severe supply-side bottleneck or demand collapse that forces margin compression and inventory write-downs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below 105.0, or the signal produces a positive predictive IC in more than 40% of out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong employment growth alongside accelerating inflation signals an overheating economy that prompts aggressive Federal Reserve policy tightening, ultimately weighing on equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint rise in PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized while housing starts remain below 1.2 million units, supply constraints in the housing sector spill over into broader construction and consumer durables, creating inflationary pressure that dampens equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: high growth with housing supply shortage
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not negatively correlated with the GDPC1/HOUST divergence, or the mechanism fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing over a 2-year period.
